title: Faramea suerrensis
taxonomy: Faramea suerrensis belongs to the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the madder family. This family comprises over 600 genera and more than 13,000 species, many of which are known for their ornamental and medicinal properties. Faramea suerrensis is a species of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, specifically in the countries of Costa Rica, Guatemala, Honduras, and Panama. The classification of Faramea suerrensis further breaks down into the genus Faramea, which contains about 100 species of flowering plants. The classification is based on morphological characteristics and molecular analysis, with the most recent classification being based on molecular phylogenetic studies.
uses: Faramea suerrensis is known to have various medicinal properties, with its roots and leaves being used in traditional medicine for their potential health benefits. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various ailments.
